text: Nonoperative management of hallux rigidus in elite athletes includes anti-inflammatory medications, injection therapies, shoewear modifications, and orthotic devices as mainstay conservative options.
Joint-sparing cheilectomy provides a predictable return to sport for elite athletes with hallux rigidus.
A proximal phalangeal osteotomy can be added to cheilectomy when additional correction is necessary for hallux rigidus in athletes.
Arthroplasty or arthrodesis for hallux rigidus in elite athletes yields less predictable return-to-sport outcomes compared to joint-sparing cheilectomy.
Surgical management of hallux rigidus should be considered when the condition limits an athlete's athletic performance despite conservative treatment.
